findings.criterion: Gherkin steps are implemented with concrete bindings and a drag-and-drop mechanism; Then-steps assert DOM content at the required selectors.

evidence.quote: When the user drags the product item with data-testid "product-item-3" and drops it into the drop area with data-testid "drop-area"

findings.reasoning: The Given steps instantiate a WebDriver and wait for the product element with data-testid 'product-item-3'. The When step invokes a helper that constructs a DataTransfer and dispatches dragstart/dragenter/dragover/drop/dragend events via execute_script, providing a valid browser-side drag simulation. The Then steps locate '#div1 .box2', '#div1 .box3', and '#div1 .box1' and assert their text for title, price, and quantity respectively, matching the requirement that unit price appears in .box3.

findings.criterion: Potential identifier mapping false-positive flagged in static facts
findings.reasoning: Static facts list 'drop-area' as missing from code, but the When-step implementation is parameterized (uses '{cart_id}' and locates f"[data-testid='{cart_id}']"). This may be a false-positive in the static mapping; nevertheless, ensure the test runtime actually supplies the 'drop-area' data-testid in the page under test so the parameterized selector resolves at execution time.
findings.suggested_fix: Confirm the AUT contains an element with data-testid='drop-area' or update scenario/code to use the same test id string to avoid mapping ambiguity.
